notes: Non-numeric source notes only; the locked PNG is the visual ground truth.

description: Input-source blocks feed benchmark asset construction from HERB-derived herb-target associations, HERB-derived herb-compound context, ChEMBL-derived compound-target context, and target-target context.
notes: Source provenance is represented schematically rather than as a numeric table.

description: Sequential quality-control blocks show herb identity normalization, target harmonization with UMLS, duplication and ambiguity screening, and benchmark eligibility filters.
notes: These are process steps in the construction workflow.

description: The asset summary block lists frozen categories for herbs, targets, corrected herb-target positives, formal eligible positives after quarantine, herb-compound edges, high-strict compound-target edges, and target-target unordered pairs.
notes: Numeric values remain embedded in the locked figure image and are not re-tabulated here.

description: The dashed quarantine block notes that H0847 was quarantined and its positive pairs were excluded from all splits under protocol v1.0.
The top schematic depicts singleton and multi-member split-blocking groups used to define leakage-control units.
notes: The ellipsis indicates continuation through additional groups.

description: Train, validation, and test blocks are shown as group-aware partitions where no split-blocking group crosses partitions.
notes: This defines the split-blocking logic for benchmark construction.

description: The dashed Group X example and red cross indicate that the same split-blocking group cannot appear in both validation and test partitions.
Held-out herbs are excluded from the training graph and evaluated only within their assigned validation or sealed-test boundary.
The statistics block summarizes split-blocking group composition and identifies context-only herbs excluded from splits.
notes: Included as explanatory support; numeric entries are not reproduced as source data.

description: The principles block states that held-out herbs are excluded from the training graph, split-blocking groups do not cross partitions, and split blocking controls the exact normalized-name identity route rather than medicinal-material equivalence.
notes: Defines the scope and limitation of leakage control.

description: The vertical workflow proceeds from protocol freeze to validation-only tuning, final five-seed training, registered validation-only analyses, validation-only robustness checks, sealed-test access precheck, and one-time sealed test.
notes: Sealed test is reserved for final evaluation only.

description: The guardrail column states that test labels remain unseen before final test and that no post-test tuning, no post-test model selection, and no post-test design change are allowed.
The dashed sealed-test summary block identifies the final reporting boundary for test herbs, recorded positive pairs, and candidate targets.
notes: Numeric entries remain in the locked image and are not re-tabulated here.
